runs.text: Inline and Floating Images
This document exercises both inline and floating image placement.
An inline image appears within the
text flow, while a floating image
uses anchor positioning with square text wrap.
Inline Image
The inline image appears below in its own paragraph.
Text continues after the inline image. The image should be embedded
in the normal document flow.
Floating Image
The floating image in this section uses square text wrapping.
Surrounding text should flow around the
image boundaries.
This tests the layout engine's ability to handle anchor-positioned
objects with wrap
constraints.
Paragraph 1. Image fidelity filler paragraph for pagination. This content is intentionally long enough to
exercise line wrapping, paragraph spacing, and page flow across multiple pages.
